data.title: California Resources Is Maintained at Overweight by JP Morgan
data.description: (13:23 GMT) California Resources Price Target Raised to $66.00/Share From $64.00 by JP Morgan

data.title: Ottawa to ensure low-paid Canadians automatically get benefits, says Carney
data.description:
OTTAWA, Oct 10 (Reuters) - Canada will launch automatic federal benefits for more than 5.5 million low-income Canadians, ensuring vulnerable members of society get the supports they are entitled to, Prime Minister Mark Carney said on Friday. "When this program is fully operational, it will pay out over hundreds of millions dollars in benefits in the next few years that would otherwise be left unclaimed," he said in a speech at a school in Ottawa. (Reporting by David Ljunggren, editing by Maria Cheng) ((Reuters Ottawa editorial; david.ljunggren@tr.com))

Keywords: CANADA POLITICS/BENEFITS

data.title: Team Montlick Race Team Captures Inaugural Monster Energy Triple Crown Championship
data.description:
ATLANTA, GA / ACCESS Newswire / October 10, 2025 / Team Montlick, sponsored by Montlick Injury Attorneys, has been crowned the inaugural Monster Energy Triple Crown Champion in the 2025 P1 Offshore Series.

The Triple Crown spans six high-speed races across three major venues - New Orleans, LA; Sheboygan, WI; and St. Petersburg, FL - with teams earning points based on finishing positions in each event. By consistently placing among the top boats, Team Montlick secured the Triple Crown Championship in the final race of the series.

"Every race brought new challenges-from unpredictable waters to fierce rivalries-but the team never wavered," said Logan Adan, driver of Team Montlick. Winning the first-ever Triple Crown is a huge honor."

This on-water victory reflects the values the Montlick name represents: resilience, precision, and relentless commitment to excellence.

"Championships aren't won by chance - they're built on discipline, trust, and preparation," said Nikki Montlick, President of Montlick Injury Attorneys. "Just like our firm, the team is committed to achieving excellence in the face of challenges."

As the first Triple Crown champions, Team Montlick has set a new standard for performance and consistency. The Triple Crown series is sanctioned by the American Power Boat Association (APBA) and organized by P1 Offshore, which has showcased premier powerboat racing events for more than 20 years across the United States and internationally.

About Montlick Injury Attorneys

Founded in 1984, Montlick Injury Attorneys is an Atlanta-based personal injury law firm representing clients nationwide. Known for integrity and client-centered advocacy, Montlick has secured billions in compensation for injury victims.

Contact Information

Jenny Harty

770-265-7404

jharty@montlick.com

SOURCE: Montlick Injury Attorneys

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Enertopia Corp Provides Oxyhydrogen Gas Safety Video
data.description:
On October 10, 2025, Enertopia Corp released a video highlighting the safety and environmental benefits of oxyhydrogen gas.

Key Highlights:

  • Video compares natural gas/propane with oxyhydrogen gas.
  • Oxyhydrogen is renewable, emitting only water vapor when burned.
  • Propane leaks can cause violent explosions; oxyhydrogen is safer.
  • Future videos will address safe storage and use of oxyhydrogen.
  • Oxyhydrogen offers a safer alternative for home appliances.

Original SEC Filing: Enertopia Corp. [ ENRT ] - 8-K - Oct. 10, 2025

Disclaimer
AI-powered summaries may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source.

data.title: Dollar Weakens After Winning Streak — Market Talk
data.description: The dollar takes a break and is on path to snap a four-day winning streak as the government shutdown continues with no end in sight. "We're still flying blind," BBH analysts write about the lack of data due to the shutdown. The dollar's recent gains, they say, "largely reflect external headwinds," such as France's political crisis and the election in Japan of pro-stimulus leadership. "That suggests USD is prone to sharp pullback as soon as global conditions normalize." The WSJ Dollar Index slips 0.1%. The dollar weakens 0.4% against the yen and 0.1% versus the euro. Treasury yields are also falling. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: Shutdown tenth day: Wall Street equity indexes set for slightly higher open
data.description:
Key points:
  • Wall Street stock index futures edge up
  • Europe's STOXX 600 slips ~0.2%
  • Dollar flat; gold, bitcoin rise; crude falls ~2%
  • U.S. 10-year Treasury yield falls to 4.09%

SHUTDOWN TENTH DAY: WALL STREET EQUITY INDEXES SET FOR SLIGHTLY HIGHER OPEN

U.S. main stock indexes were set to open slightly higher on Friday, after ending the previous session in red, as the bull market rally hits a three-year mark on Sunday.

With the U.S. government shutdown entering its tenth day and expected to extend into the next week, investor risk sentiment still remained intact, fueled by AI optimism and bets of further rate cuts, despite the unavailability of official economic data.

However, as market fears of a bubble percolate, the upcoming corporate earnings will be crucial for the momentum of U.S. equity markets.

U.S. Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said on CNBC's Squawk Box that private data shows the job market remains weak and buttresses the case for further rate cuts, but added the central bank needs only to move in "cautious" quarter percentage point steps as it evaluates the economy.

On the data front, a preliminary reading of the University of Michigan's consumer sentiment survey is due later in the day.

Among stocks, Qualcomm fell more than 1% after China's market regulator said the country has launched an antitrust investigation into the semiconductor manufacturer over its acquisition of Israel's Autotalks.

Levi Strauss's shares dropped nearly 9% in premarket trading after the denim maker's annual profit forecast failed to meet investor expectations.

Gold's rally lost some steam as it fell below the $4000/oz mark on Friday, but rebounded from earlier losses.

Here's a market snapshot at 9:03 a.m. ET:

(Siddarth S)

*****

EARLIER ON LIVE MARKETS:

data.title: Political and General News Events from October 10
data.description:
For other diaries, please see:

Political and General News

Top Economic Events

Emerging Markets Economic Events

Government Debt Auctions

U.S. Federal Reserve

Today in Washington

--------------------------------------------------------------------

This diary is filed daily. ** Indicates new events

--------------------------------------------------------------------

FRIDAY, OCTOBER 10

** ANKARA - Turkey will host Iraqi Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Fuad Hussein, who will hold a press conference with Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan in Ankara.

** BERN - Swiss Foreign Minister Ignazio Cassis meets with China's foreign affairs minister Wang - 1400 GMT.

** PHNOM PENH - Prime Minister of the Commonwealth of Dominica, Dr. Roosevelt Skerrit visits in Phnom Penh for a working visit in Cambodia (Final day)

PYONGYANG - China's Premier Li Qiang and Vietnam's top leader, To Lam, will visit North Korea to mark celebrations of the 80th anniversary of its ruling Workers Party of Korea (To Oct 11)

AUCKLAND, New Zealand - Singapore's Prime Minister Lawrence Wong will visit New Zealand. (To Oct. 11)

DELHI - Afghan Foreign Minister Amir Khan Muttaqi of the Taliban government is set to visit India (To Oct 16).

CANBERRA – Indian Defence Minister Rajnath Singh will be on a two-day official visit to Australia at the invitation of Deputy Prime Minister and Defence Minister of Australia Richard Marles. (Final day)

COPENHAGEN - Denmark hosts an informal meeting of the EU's development ministers. (Final day)

DUSHANBE - President of Belarus Aleksandr Lukashenko visits Dushanbe. In Tajikistan's capital city the Belarusian leader will take part in a session of the CIS Heads of State Council on 10 October. (Final day)

DUSHANBE - Tajikistan President Emomali Rakhmon hosts the second Russia-Central Asia summit. Russian President Vladimir Putin along with presidents of Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan and Kazakhstan will attend (Final day)

KUALA LUMPUR - Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim presents his government's budget spending plan for 2026 to parliament - 0800 GMT.

BEIJING - At the invitation of Chinese President Xi Jinping, President John Dramani Mahama of Ghana, will pay a state visit to China.

LUXEMBOURG - European Union finance ministers meet to discuss a European Commission proposal to extend a 140 billion euro loan to Ukraine using immobilised Russian central bank assets. They will also discuss the Commission’s recent proposals to update the system of sources of revenue for the EU budget and its recent proposal to update the structure and minimum rates of excise duty applied to manufactured tobacco and tobacco-related products in the EU - 0700 GMT"

GLOBAL - World mental health day.

GLOBAL - World Day against death penalty.

GLOBAL - World Sight Day.

FIJI – 54th anniversary of independence.

PYONGYANG - Laotian President Thongloun Sisoulith will visit North Korea at the invitation of leader Kim Jong Un for celebrations commemorating North Korea's ruling party.

LUXEMBOURG – EU Economic and Financial Affairs Council meeting.

data.title: StandardAero Initiated at Buy by Truist Securities
Elastic Announces $500 Million Buyback Plan. The Stock Is Climbing Sharply. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nate Wolf

Shares of Elastic rose sharply Friday after the search and data-analytics company announced a share repurchase plan and unveiled ambitious financial targets at its yearly analyst day.

The Elastic board authorized up to $500 million in buybacks with no expiration date, citing its confidence in the company's business and balance sheet. The company aims to complete at least half of the authorized repurchases in fiscal 2026.

Elastic also unveiled a medium-term financial framework to reach 20% annual revenue growth plus a 20% adjusted free-cash flow margin — hitting the so-called Rule of 40.

In the immediate future, management lifted its fiscal 2026 revenue forecast to between $1.697 billion and $1.703 billion, up a tick from a previous range of $1.679 billion to $1.689 billion. Second-quarter revenue is expected to total $417 million to $419 million, $2 million higher at the midpoint than the company's past estimate.

Elastic stock was up 12% to $91.70 in premarket trading Friday. Shares closed Thursday down 18% for the year despite Elastic posting a series of earnings beats.

Wall Street seemed to come away from analyst day optimistic about Elastic's outlook.

"The medium-term financial framework provided by Elastic is bullish and driven by [artificial intelligence]," wrote Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Thomas Blakey in a research note. The firm reiterated a Neutral rating on Elastic shares and lifted its price target to $94 from $92.

Elastic's Rule of 40 target over the next two to three years was above consensus expectations, Guggenheim Securities said in a research note. The firm noted that Elastic's base of AI customers is expanding at an outsized rate, which should help the company reach its goal.

Guggenheim reiterated a Buy rating and a $122 price target on the stock.

Write to Nate Wolf at nate.wolf@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Canada 10-Year Government Bond Yield Little Changed at 3.19%
data.description:
The yield on the 10-year Canadian government bond has been fluctuating around 3.19% in October, near its May-lows, as traders continue to monitor economic, political, and monetary developments.

Market expectations for another 25bps rate cut by the Bank of Canada later this month have fallen to 57% from 72%, following stronger-than-expected labour market data showing a 60K increase in employment, while the unemployment rate unexpectedly held steady at 7.1%.

On the political front, Prime Minister Carney met with US President Trump to continue tariff negotiations.

Carney said Canada “will get an even better deal,” while Trump pledged to treat Canada fairly but was less committal regarding a broader continental trade deal that includes Mexico.

In addition, Carney’s government is set to unveil its first budget on November 4, where new investment-focused stimulus measures are expected to be announced.

The government has indicated plans to expand the fiscal deficit to around 3% of GDP.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Waiting for Weather — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. natural gas futures are falling for a third day in a row as storage levels remain above average and strong weather-driven demand is lacking. Storage 4.5% to 5% above the five-year average is "a little high" moving into the back end of October, says Dennis Kissler of BOK Financial. The recent run-up in prices has been more due to profit-taking than new buying, he says. "Every time it gets to $3.30 or $3.50, the market just keeps backing off because the traders want to see storage move back below that five-year average, or approach it." And with production running high, that will need a weather event as a catalyst, he adds. Nymex natural gas is down 3% at $3.172/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)
